title: How One Small Pause Builds a Godly Man's Self-Control
caption:
Self-control isn't the willpower to win a fight in the moment. It's what you plant in the gap long before the moment shows up.

Every failure you regret had a gap in it. Between the text and the send. The scroll and the search bar. The first drink and the fourth. You never fell straight into it, you walked through a gap you didn't notice.

So name your gap. Put one move in it before you need it, a verse, a text to a brother, a lap around the block. Practice the pause on the small stuff, the extra scroll, the extra minute, so it's ready when it's not small anymore.

But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control. (Galatians 5:22-23)

Self-control isn't something you grit your teeth into. It's something that grows because the Spirit's in you.

Save this for the next time the gap opens up.

#faith #christianmen #selfcontrol #discipline #godlymen #manofgod #scripture
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
